mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly growth in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals robust labor demand and consumer income expansion, which drives equity valuations higher through increased corporate earnings expectations and consumer spending power over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:April 2017 PAYEMS at 145858k reflects 6+ months of consistent monthly gains
regime_hint: post-crisis expansion with stable labor market
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive PAYEMS growth fails to produce positive 20d SPX returns in > 55% of instances where PAYEMS growth exceeds 150k/month.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 1.0% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) shows stable or declining momentum, equity risk premiums compress as real rates stay accommodative, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors rotate into equities seeking yield.
evidence_refs: fact:April 2017 FEDFUNDS at 0.79% and CPIAUCSL at 243.752 indicate accommodative real rates
regime_hint: low-rate monetary accommodation with contained inflation
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 45% of instances where both conditions are met, or Spearman rank correlation between (FEDFUNDS, CPIAUCSL) interaction term and 60d returns is < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening forward demand and construction activity, which predicts negative equity returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations downward and reduce cyclical equity exposure.
evidence_refs: fact:February 2017 HOUST at 1288k represents baseline for trigger evaluation
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of instances where HOUST declines > 5% MoM, or Rank IC between HOUST momentum and 5d returns exceeds 0.02 in absolute value.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 1.5% annualized signals expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side strength, which supports corporate profit margins and equity valuations over 20 trading days as cost pressures remain contained.
evidence_refs: fact:February 2017 INDPRO at 103.7332 (index 2017=100) reflects modest supply expansion
regime_hint: synchronized global growth with stable input costs
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in > 52% of instances where 3-month INDPRO growth exceeds 1.5%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) move together as joint indicators of consumer and construction demand; when both decline simultaneously, it signals broad-based demand weakness that produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as earnings revisions turn negative.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 2% month-over-month in same month
evidence_refs: fact:March 2017 RSAFS at 470844M and HOUST at 1288k show demand baseline
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 48% of instances where both RSAFS and HOUST decline > 2% MoM, or interaction term (RSAFS decline × HOUST decline) shows Rank IC < 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 4.7% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer confidence, which produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors reduce equity allocations in anticipation of earnings pressure from lower consumer spending.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.4 percentage points in 2 months
evidence_refs: fact:March 2017 UNRATE at 4.5% provides baseline for trigger evaluation
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.4pp in 2 months, or Rank IC between UNRATE change and 60d returns is < 0.05 in absolute value.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% annualized while the federal funds rate remains below 1.5%, real rates turn negative, triggering equity repricing as investors demand higher risk premiums; this produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:March 2017 CPIAUCSL at 243.752 and FEDFUNDS at 0.79% show current real rate environment
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 52% of instances where both conditions are met, or Spearman rank correlation between (CPIAUCSL - FEDFUNDS) spread and 20d returns is < 0.04 in absolute value.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured at quarterly frequency above 2.5% annualized signals sustained economic expansion that supports equity valuations; this always-on trend mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings growth remains robust.
evidence_refs: fact:Q4 2016 GDPC1 at 16813.3B (chained 2017 dollars) reflects post-election expansion
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 48% of instances where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5% annualized, or Rank IC between GDPC1 growth rate and 60d returns is < 0.06 over any 3-year rolling window.
